theorem_statement:
Glassey blow-up argument for the focusing mass-critical nonlinear Schrodinger equation.

Consider a sufficiently regular solution \(u(t,x)\) to the focusing mass-critical NLS on \(\mathbb{R}^d\):

\[
i\partial_t u+\Delta u=-|u|^{4/d}u.
\]

Assume:

- \(u_0\in H^1(\mathbb{R}^d)\),

- \(|x|u_0\in L^2(\mathbb{R}^d)\),

- the energy \(E(u_0)\) is negative, where

\[
E(u)=\frac12\int_{\mathbb{R}^d}|\nabla u|^2\,dx
-\frac{1}{2+4/d}\int_{\mathbb{R}^d}|u|^{2+4/d}\,dx.
\]

Then the maximal forward existence time \(T\) is finite and

\[
\|u(t)\|_{H^1(\mathbb{R}^d)}\to\infty
\quad\text{as }t\uparrow T.
\]

nodes.title: Glassey blow-up for focusing mass-critical NLS
nodes.informal_statement: Under the assumptions \(u_0\in H^1\), \(|x|u_0\in L^2\), and \(E(u_0)<0\), the forward maximal lifespan \(T\) of the solution to \(i\partial_tu+\Delta u=-|u|^{4/d}u\) is finite and \(\|u(t)\|_{H^1(\mathbb{R}^d)}\to\infty\) as \(t\uparrow T\).
nodes.informal_proof_text: Define the variance \(V(t)=\int |x|^2|u(t,x)|^2\,dx\). The virial computation gives \(V''(t)=16E(u(t))=16E(u_0)<0\), so the variance is strictly concave with negative constant second derivative. Since \(V(t)\ge 0\), this forces a finite time \(T\) at which \(V(t)\to 0\) as \(t\uparrow T\). A weighted inequality together with mass conservation gives \(\|u_0\|_{L^2}^2\le \frac{2}{d}\sqrt{V(t)}\,\|\nabla u(t)\|_{L^2}\). Because the mass is fixed and \(\sqrt{V(t)}\to 0\), one gets \(\|\nabla u(t)\|_{L^2}\to\infty\), hence also \(\|u(t)\|_{H^1}\to\infty\). Therefore the solution cannot persist globally forward in \(H^1\), so the maximal forward existence time is finite.

nodes.title: Virial second-derivative identity
nodes.informal_proof_text:
Differentiate \(V(t)\) once and use \(u_t=i\Delta u+i|u|^{4/d}u\). The nonlinear contribution in the first derivative is real before multiplication by \(i\), so its real part vanishes, and integration by parts yields
\[
V'(t)=4\operatorname{Im}\int x\cdot \nabla u\,\overline u\,dx.
\]
Differentiate again and substitute \(u_t=i\Delta u+i|u|^{4/d}u\) and \(\overline{u_t}=-i\Delta\overline u-i|u|^{4/d}\overline u\). Splitting linear and nonlinear terms, a standard integration-by-parts computation gives for the linear part
\[
4\operatorname{Re}\int x\cdot \nabla\Delta u\,\overline u\,dx-4\operatorname{Re}\int x\cdot \nabla u\,\Delta\overline u\,dx=8\int |\nabla u|^2\,dx.
\]
For the nonlinear part, rewrite the real-part contribution as a divergence term and integrate by parts using \(\operatorname{div}(x)=d\), obtaining
\[
-\frac{16}{2+4/d}\int |u|^{2+4/d}\,dx.
\]
Hence
\[
V''(t)=8\int |\nabla u|^2\,dx-\frac{16}{2+4/d}\int |u|^{2+4/d}\,dx=16E(u(t)).
\]
By conservation of energy, \(E(u(t))=E(u_0)\), so \(V''(t)=16E(u_0)<0\).

nodes.title: Weighted \(L^2\) inequality
nodes.informal_proof_text:
Use \(\operatorname{div}(x)=d\) and integrate by parts:
\[
\|f\|_{L^2}^2=\int |f|^2\,dx=\frac1d\int (\operatorname{div}x)|f|^2\,dx=-\frac1d\int x\cdot \nabla(|f|^2)\,dx=-\frac{2}{d}\operatorname{Re}\int x\cdot \nabla f\,\overline f\,dx.
\]
Then apply \(|\operatorname{Re} z|\le |z|\) and Cauchy-Schwarz to obtain
\[
\|f\|_{L^2}^2\le \frac{2}{d}\,\|xf\|_{L^2}\,\|\nabla f\|_{L^2}.
\]

nodes.title: Negative virial forces finite-time collapse of the variance
nodes.informal_statement: Since \(V''(t)=16E(u_0)<0\) and \(V(t)\ge 0\), there exists a finite positive time \(T\) such that \(V(T)=0\), equivalently \(V(t)\to 0\) as \(t\uparrow T\).
nodes.informal_proof_text: The identity \(V''(t)=16E(u_0)<0\) shows that, on its interval of existence, \(V\) is a concave quadratic with negative leading coefficient. Such a function cannot stay nonnegative for all sufficiently large positive times. But by definition \(V(t)=\int |x|^2|u(t,x)|^2\,dx\ge 0\). Therefore the forward evolution cannot continue with positive variance forever; by continuity there is a finite positive time \(T\) at which the nonnegative variance reaches \(0\), and hence \(V(t)\to 0\) as \(t\uparrow T\).

nodes.title: Variance collapse and conserved mass force gradient blow-up
nodes.informal_statement:
Let \(M=\|u_0\|_{L^2(\mathbb{R}^d)}\). Then
\[
M^2\le \frac{2}{d}\sqrt{V(t)}\,\|\nabla u(t)\|_{L^2(\mathbb{R}^d)}.
\]
If \(V(t)\to 0\) as \(t\uparrow T\), it follows that \(\|\nabla u(t)\|_{L^2}\to\infty\), and therefore \(\|u(t)\|_{H^1}\to\infty\).
nodes.informal_proof_text:
Apply the weighted inequality to \(f=u(t)\). By conservation of mass, \(\|u(t)\|_{L^2}=\|u_0\|_{L^2}=:M\), so
\[
M^2\le \frac{2}{d}\,\|xu(t)\|_{L^2}\,\|\nabla u(t)\|_{L^2}=\frac{2}{d}\sqrt{V(t)}\,\|\nabla u(t)\|_{L^2}.
\]
As \(t\uparrow T\), the quantity \(M>0\) stays fixed while \(\sqrt{V(t)}\to 0\). Thus the product estimate can hold only if \(\|\nabla u(t)\|_{L^2}\to\infty\). Since
\[
\|u(t)\|_{H^1}^2=\|u(t)\|_{L^2}^2+\|\nabla u(t)\|_{L^2}^2
\]
and the \(L^2\) norm is conserved, this also implies \(\|u(t)\|_{H^1}\to\infty\).
nodes.formalization_rationale: Best first local target: it is inferentially important for the main theorem, technically self-contained once mass conservation and variance collapse are available, and it packages the key coercive estimate into the actual blow-up conclusion. Formalizing this node discharges more real burden than the standalone weighted inequality, while avoiding the substantially heavier PDE/virial computation in the virial identity.

formal_artifact.lean_statement:
theorem gradient_blowup_of_variance_collapse
    {d M : ℝ} (hd : 0 < d) (hM : 0 < M)
    {V grad h1 : ℕ → ℝ}
    (hbound : ∀ n, M ^ 2 ≤ (2 / d) * Real.sqrt (V n) * grad n)
    (hV_nonneg : ∀ n, 0 ≤ V n)
    (hV : Tendsto V atTop (𝓝 0))
    (hh1 : ∀ n, h1 n ^ 2 = M ^ 2 + grad n ^ 2) :
    Tendsto grad atTop atTop ∧ Tendsto h1 atTop atTop
formal_artifact.lean_code:
import Mathlib.Analysis.SpecialFunctions.Sqrt
import Mathlib.Topology.Instances.ENNReal
import Mathlib.Topology.Algebra.Order
import Mathlib

open Filter Topology
open scoped Topology

theorem gradient_blowup_of_variance_collapse
    {d M : ℝ} (hd : 0 < d) (hM : 0 < M)
    {V grad h1 : ℕ → ℝ}
    (hbound : ∀ n, M ^ 2 ≤ (2 / d) * Real.sqrt (V n) * grad n)
    (hV_nonneg : ∀ n, 0 ≤ V n)
    (hV : Tendsto V atTop (𝓝 0))
    (hh1 : ∀ n, h1 n ^ 2 = M ^ 2 + grad n ^ 2) :
    Tendsto grad atTop atTop ∧ Tendsto h1 atTop atTop := by
  have hd2 : 0 < 2 / d := by positivity
  have hM2 : 0 < M ^ 2 := by nlinarith
  have hgrad_nonneg : ∀ n, 0 ≤ grad n := by
    intro n
    have hsqrt_nonneg : 0 ≤ Real.sqrt (V n) := Real.sqrt_nonneg _
    have hprod_nonneg : 0 ≤ (2 / d) * Real.sqrt (V n) := by positivity
    by_contra hneg
    have hlt : (2 / d) * Real.sqrt (V n) * grad n < 0 := by
      nlinarith
    linarith [hbound n]
  have hgrad : Tendsto grad atTop atTop := by
    rw [tendsto_atTop.2]
    intro b
    let ε : ℝ := (M ^ 2 / ((2 / d) * (b + 1))) ^ 2
    have hden : 0 < (2 / d) * (b + 1) := by
      have hb1 : 0 < b + 1 := by linarith
      positivity
    have hε : 0 < ε := by
      dsimp [ε]
      positivity
    have hVevent : ∀ᶠ n in atTop, V n < ε := by
      have h := (tendsto_order.1 hV).2 0 ε hε
      simpa using h
    filter_upwards [hVevent] with n hn
    have hsqrt_lt : Real.sqrt (V n) < M ^ 2 / ((2 / d) * (b + 1)) := by
      have hnn : 0 ≤ V n := hV_nonneg n
      have hrhs_nonneg : 0 ≤ M ^ 2 / ((2 / d) * (b + 1)) := by positivity
      apply (Real.sqrt_lt_iff).2
      constructor
      · exact hnn
      · have := hn
        dsimp [ε] at this
        simpa [sq] using this
    have hmul_pos : 0 < (2 / d) * Real.sqrt (V n) := by
      positivity
    have hle1 : M ^ 2 < (2 / d) * Real.sqrt (V n) * (b + 1) := by
      have := mul_lt_mul_of_pos_left hsqrt_lt hd2
      have hb1 : 0 < b + 1 := by linarith
      have := mul_lt_mul_of_pos_right this hb1
      field_simp at this
      simpa [mul_assoc, mul_left_comm, mul_comm] using this
    have hbg : b < grad n := by
      by_contra hnot
      have hle_grad : grad n ≤ b := by linarith
      have hle2 : (2 / d) * Real.sqrt (V n) * grad n ≤ (2 / d) * Real.sqrt (V n) * b := by
        gcongr
      have hlt2 : (2 / d) * Real.sqrt (V n) * b < (2 / d) * Real.sqrt (V n) * (b + 1) := by
        nlinarith [hmul_pos]
      linarith [hbound n, hle2, hle1]
    linarith
  have hh1_nonneg : ∀ n, 0 ≤ h1 n := by
    intro n
    have hsq_nonneg : 0 ≤ h1 n ^ 2 := sq_nonneg _
    by_contra hneg
    nlinarith
  have hh1_tendsto : Tendsto h1 atTop atTop := by
    rw [tendsto_atTop.2]
    intro b
    have hgrad_event : ∀ᶠ n in atTop, grad n ≥ b + 1 := by
      simpa using (tendsto_atTop.2 hgrad (b + 1))
    filter_upwards [hgrad_event] with n hn
    have hsum : h1 n ^ 2 = M ^ 2 + grad n ^ 2 := hh1 n
    have hlarge : b ^ 2 < h1 n ^ 2 := by
      have hgb : b < grad n := by linarith
      have hgb_sq : b ^ 2 < grad n ^ 2 := by
        have hgn : 0 ≤ grad n := hgrad_nonneg n
        nlinarith
      nlinarith [hsum]
    have hnneg : 0 ≤ h1 n := hh1_nonneg n
    by_contra hle
    have : h1 n ≤ b := by linarith
    have hsquare_le : h1 n ^ 2 ≤ b ^ 2 := by
      nlinarith
    linarith
  exact ⟨hgrad, hh1_tendsto⟩

edges.label: uses
edges.explanation: Finite-time failure of the variance is the mechanism producing a finite forward blow-up time.

edges.label: uses
edges.explanation: Once the variance collapses, the weighted estimate forces the \(H^1\) norm to diverge.

edges.label: depends_on
edges.explanation: The concavity argument starts from the explicit identity \(V''(t)=16E(u_0)<0\).

edges.label: depends_on
edges.explanation: The lower bound relating mass, variance, and gradient comes from the weighted \(L^2\) inequality.

edges.label: depends_on
edges.explanation: To force \(\|\nabla u(t)\|_{L^2}\to\infty\), one needs \(V(t)\to 0\) at a finite time.
